form_analysis.form_header_text: Fiscal Year 2027 Programmatic Funding Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: If you have any questions, please email Appropriations@ossoff.senate.gov. Please certify that all the information included in your application is accurate and that it is made in accordance with the applicable rules, fiduciary requirements and bylaws of the organization. Submitting this application does NOT guarantee your programmatic request will receive funding or be included as bill or report language.

sections.description: Please confirm that this is a non-defense Programmatic Request. By selecting "Yes", you are acknowledging that this request is for non-defense Programmatic Funding and/or report language for Fiscal Year 2027, and is not a request for Congressionally Directed Spending.

fields.label: qi_2$
fields.help_text: Confirm that this is a non-defense Programmatic Request.
fields.rationale: This is a non-defense programmatic funding request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate, which is an existing civilian agency program.

fields.label: Prefix
RequiredFirst Name
MI
RequiredLast Name
RequiredTitle
RequiredName of Organization making this request
RequiredBusiness Address Line 1
Address Line 2
RequiredCity
RequiredState
RequiredZip
RequiredPrimary Email - Email Address to Receive the Confirmation Receipt on Submission
RequiredConfirm Email
RequiredCell Phone
RequiredRequesting Organization Name
If not applicable, please type "NA"
RequiredOrganization DesignationIs the requesting organization a 501c3 non-profit, non-profit that is not a 501c3, for-profit, state government, local government entity, or public utility?
fields.rationale: This field requires the specific legal status of the submitter's organization, which is not provided in the context.

fields.label: RequiredPlease describe your Georgia footprint.
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science obligated $33,777,066 in Georgia between FY2023 and FY2025, supporting 117 grants and 45 contracts across the state.
* Georgia Tech Research Corp is a cornerstone partner, with active research awards exceeding $35 million for astronaut safety, lunar resource studies, and planetary exploration.
* Local industrial partners including Georgia Thermal Products LLC (Cumming) and American Material Handling, Inc. (Watkinsville) provide specialized fabrication and ground support.
* Funding supports high-skilled research positions at Georgia State University and the University of Georgia, advancing fundamental science and workforce development.
fields.rationale: The local spending data confirms over $33.7M in obligations (FY23-25) and lists Georgia Tech, Georgia State, UGA, and multiple small businesses as key recipients.

fields.label: Provide a brief, 2-3 sentence summary of your previous appropriations requests, including the relevant Fiscal Year. If not applicable, please type "NA"
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding prior submission history.

sections.description: Please fill out this section regardless of what type of request(s) you will be making.

fields.label: RequiredUnder which appropriations bill does this request fall?Select the appropriate subcommittee for this programmatic request. Please reach out to Ossoff Office staff via email if you are unsure.
options: Commerce, Justice, Science and Related Agencies
Housing and Urban Development and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding is under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science (CJS) subcommittee.

fields.label: RequiredDepartmentSelect the Department through which you are requesting funding.
options: Department of Health and Human Services
Department of Housing and Urban Development
fields.rationale: NASA is an independent agency, not a department. Since NASA is not listed in the standard department options, 'Other' is the appropriate selection.

fields.label: If not applicable, please type "NA"
fields.help_text: If Department is 'Other', please list here.
fields.rationale: The request falls under NASA's Science Mission Directorate, not a generic 'Other' department category.

fields.label: RequiredAccount NameProvide the account through which you are requesting funding.
fields.rationale: The authoritative account name for this request within NASA is 'Science'.

fields.label: RequiredProgram NameProvide the name of the federal program for which you are requesting funding or report language.
fields.rationale: The Science Mission Directorate (SMD) is the specific programmatic entity for which funding is being requested.

fields.label: qi_19$
fields.help_text: Are you recommending a specific programmatic funding amount for FY27?
fields.rationale: The request specifically recommends a programmatic funding amount of $9 billion for FY27 for NASA Science.

fields.label: RequiredFY27 Funding Request- Please include the whole amount your organization is requesting for FY27 without decimals or abbreviations.- If not applicable, please type "NA"
fields.rationale: This reflects the specific funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate in FY27 as provided in the reference values.

fields.label: RequiredIf you are not requesting a specific dollar amount, what are you requesting?If you are seeking robust funding or something similar that lacks a numeric value, please indicate that in this space.
fields.rationale: The request includes a specific dollar amount ($9,000,000,000) as specified in the funding ask.

fields.label: RequiredDid this program receive funding in the FY26 enacted Appropriations bills?- Please include the whole amount received during the FY26 appropriations process.- If not applicable, please type "NA"
fields.rationale: This is the Enacted Total for the Science account in FY2026 according to H.R. 6938 as specified in the reference data.

fields.label: RequiredWhat is the funding level of the program in the FY27 President's Budget Request?- Please include the funding level of the program included in the FY27 President's Budget.- Please type $0 if the program is not included in the FY27 President's Budget.- Please type "NA" if the FY27 President's Budget has not been released at the time of submission.
fields.rationale: The reference values state that the FY2027 President's Budget Request has not yet been released.

fields.label: RequiredBy what amount does your request increase/decrease the funding level of the program in the FY27 President's Budget Request?- Please indicate the difference between the amount YOU are requesting and the amount laid out in the FY27 President's Budget in a whole number.- Please type "NA" if the FY27 President's Budget has not been released at the time of submission.
fields.rationale: The reference values state that the FY2027 President's Budget Request has not yet been released.

fields.label: RequiredPlease provide a detailed explanation of your request, including how this funding will impact Georgia communities.
fields.draft_value:
* This $9 billion request restores stability to NASA Science, protecting U.S. leadership against rapid expansion by international competitors like China.
* Funding supports 162 active grants and contracts in Georgia, fueling local innovation and sustaining the state's highly-skilled aerospace and STEM workforce.
* NASA Science delivers a 3-to-1 economic return; in FY2025 alone, $15,022,044 was obligated directly to Georgia institutions like Georgia Tech and small businesses.
* Sustained investment ensures NASA can meet peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities, driving discoveries that inspire the next generation of Georgia scientists and engineers.
fields.rationale: Combined top-line funding request ($9B) with state-specific spending data ($15M in FY25 obligations) and strategic advocacy points regarding workforce and international competition.

fields.label: RequiredHave you submitted this request to another member of the Georgia Congressional Delegation?- If so, please list the relevant members of the Georgia Congressional Delegation that have also received this request from your organization.- If not, please type "NA"
fields.draft_value:
* Reps.
* Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request.
* Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: RequiredHas this request been submitted to Members of Congress in other states?- If so, please list the relevant members of Congress that have also received this request from your organization.- If not, please type "NA"
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding other current year submissions.

fields.label: RequiredIf you wish to offer suggested language, please submit here and indicate if your request is for bill or report language.If not submitting suggested language, please type "NA"
fields.rationale: The request context indicates this is a programmatic request with no bill or report language requested.

fields.label: RequiredPlease provide a detailed explanation of your request, including how this language will impact Georgia communities.If not submitting suggested language, please type "NA"
fields.rationale: No language is being submitted, consistent with the previous field.

fields.label: RequiredHave you submitted this request to another member of the Georgia Congressional Delegation?- If so, please list the relevant members of the Georgia Congressional Delegation that have also received this request from your organization.- If not, please type "NA"
fields.draft_value:
* Reps.
* Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request.
* Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: RequiredHas this request been submitted to Members of Congress in other states?- If so, please list the relevant members of Congress that have also received this request from your organization.- If not, please type "NA"
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding other current year submissions.

sections.description: Please add any documentation necessary to provide additional information on your requests.

fields.label: Upload FileNo file chosen
fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

sections.description: Please certify that all the information included in your application is accurate and that it is made in accordance with the applicable rules, fiduciary requirements and bylaws of the organization.

fields.label: RequiredCertificationI attest that I have read these instructions and that, to the best of my knowledge, my project is eligible for Programmatic Funding.
fields.rationale: The request for NASA Science Mission Directorate funding is a standard programmatic funding request for an existing federal program.

top_contracts.description: Gsa ebuy - purchase of national crane nbt30g-2 truck crane, new from amh Inc
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Acquiring a new truck crane for ground support operations.
Fabrication of specialized protective housing for scientific instruments.

top_grants.description: Multiple year award with incremental funding for years one to five.  This proposal is entitled  radiation effects on volatiles and exploration of asteroids and lunar surfaces reveals.  This is awarded in response to cooperative agreement notice can no. nnh16zda009c  NASA solar system exploration research virtual institute sservi  cycle three 3.  The principal investigator is Dr. Thomas m. Orlando with the georgia tech research corporation git.  Project summary:  recent space exploration missions have revealed volatile deposits on several solar system objects  including the moon  Mars  Mercury  and near Earth asteroids (NEAS).  Most of these airless i.E.  Zero-atmosphere objects are under consideration by NASA as destinations for future human exploration.  These objects are bombarded by radiation (i.E. Solar wind  galactic and solar cosmic rays) and the interaction of radiation with their surfaces can alter and even produce volatiles.  More importantly  the constant radiation assault poses obvious  life-threatening  and program-limiting health risks.  As a member of the sservi  the reveals team will explore the fundamental and applied aspects correlated with radiation processing of natural regolith and man-made composite materials.  The reveals team will focus on two interdisciplinary research themes  each comprised of multiple tasks that will be carried out in an integrated systems approach.theme 1 interaction of radiation and micrometeorites with surface of airless bodies  the solar wind interaction with regolith has been implicated as a possible source of volatiles (i.E. Water) that can be trapped and sequestered in the near surface region of the moon and possibly demos  Phobos  and neas.  The relevant physical processes and driving interactions are unclear.  Therefore  theme 1 will focus on three tasks: 1) examine solar wind  micrometeorite processing of airless body surfaces and dust irradiation sources  2 develop molecular models describing radiation induced transformation of regolith  3 examine the role of charging and electric fields on volatile formation/release and 4) test solar collection panels as an in situ resource utilization strategy of producing water from regoliththeme 2 developing and testing new materials for improved safety and health during human exploration human exploration missions beyond near Earth orbit cannot occur without a validated approach to addressing astronaut health risks due to radiation.  This requires characterization of the space radiation environment  experimental validation of radiation protection methodologies  and the ability to predict and monitor the radiation absorbed by astronauts on a real-time and mission-integrated basis. As of 2016  the deep-space radiation environment is reasonably characterized  but radiation protection materials for human habitats and space-suits have not been fully developed and characterized  and real-time radiation monitors are in their infancy.  For example  real-time area radiation monitors are currently utilized on the international space station with inconsistent results  and they are not integrated with space-suits.  Development of strong  flexible and radiation protective material is also lagging.  Therefore  theme 2 will focus on assessing and mitigating human radiation risks by 1 testing regolith as a potential shielding material  2) developing  characterizing and testing novel composite materials for superior mechanical  thermal and electrical properties  3 integrating the best polymer composites as modification to existing space-suits  and 4 developing real-time low weight and low power consumption radiation monitoring devices using graphene and other 2d materials.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Researching space radiation effects and developing astronaut safety materials.

top_grants.description: Engine of innovation: how compartmentalization drives evolution of novelty and.efficiency across scales
